key_insight: LapidaryEngine enables fully conversational crystal design by using a pivot representation to translate between free-form natural language and crystal structures in both directions.
engineering_relevance: This could make materials discovery more iterative and user-driven, allowing engineers to refine target properties, compositions, and structures through dialogue instead of rigid input formats.
method: Accepts free-form natural-language requests for crystal generation
Uses a pivot representation as an intermediate shared form
Supports iterative refinement, editing, and reverse translation from crystal back to text
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstrations on insulator discovery, stability optimization, compositional modification, and structural editing, indicating evaluation across multiple material-design tasks.
watch_item: The approach depends on a pivot representation and conversational alignment, so performance may be sensitive to how well the intermediate form generalizes beyond the demonstrated tasks.
